title: Senator Josh Becker’s Interim Housing Act fast tracks the building of interim housing and scales up efforts to bring people indoors and save lives

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California has the fourth highest rate of homelessness and the highest rate of unsheltered homelessness in the nation.",
    "California’s population experiencing homelessness reached a record 181,399 individuals in 2023.",
    "SB 1395 will encourage the development of interim housing by clarifying that relocatable, non-congregate interim housing is eligible for streamlined zoning.",
    "SB 1395 will reduce construction time and costs for interim housing projects.",
    "SB 1395 extends the sunset for existing streamlining authorities to provide locals more assurance to address homelessness beyond 2026.",
    "Interim housing is considered a missing rung on the ladder to permanent housing.",
    "Interim supportive housing is a key part of the response to homelessness that can quickly transition individuals out of dangerous encampments.",
    "SB 1395 will allow attractive and cost-effective interim housing communities to be built in a matter of months."
  ]
}
```
